chunks.text: Three EECS-affiliated papers win Helmholtz Prize at ICCV 2017 - EECS at Berkeley Three papers with Berkeley authors received the Helmholtz Prize at the International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV) 2017 in Venice, Italy. This award honors papers that have stood the test of time (more than ten years after first publication) and is bestowed by the IEEE technical committee on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (PAMI). Seven papers won this year, among them: “ Recognizing action at a distance ,” by A Efros, A Berg, G Mori and J Malik, ICCV 2003; “Discovering objects and their location in images ,” by J Sivic, B Russell, A Efros, A Zisserman and W Freeman, ICCV 2005; and “ The pyramid match kernel: Discriminative classification with sets of image features ,” by K Grauman and T Darrell, ICCV 2005.” Categories Faculty Honors publications Research View Open Faculty Positions
